title: Protocol: Trial lifecycle analysis for Uganda clinical trials
body: This registry-based study will characterise the lifecycle trajectory of all interventional clinical trials listing a Ugandan site on ClinicalTrials.gov. Each trial will be classified into six lifecycle stages: registration, enrolment of at least one participant, completion, results reporting, publication, and intervention accessibility. Accessibility will be assessed by matching trial title and condition fields against WHO Essential Medicines List compounds, PEPFAR-distributed antiretrovirals, and Expanded Programme on Immunization vaccines available in the Ugandan formulary. The primary outcome is stage-by-stage survival expressed as the proportion of the original 783 registered trials reaching each stage. Secondary outcomes include stratification of lifecycle scores by sponsor type classified as Ugandan-local or foreign, by trial phase, and by disease category across fifteen predefined condition groups. Temporal trends in average lifecycle score will be examined by registration year to assess whether the research ecosystem is maturing. Limitations include restriction to a single registry and approximate keyword-based intervention-access classification.
ai_transparency: LLM assistance was used for drafting. The author reviewed and edited the manuscript and takes responsibility for the final content.

checks.name: methods before outcomes
checks.detail: Data retrieval and stage definitions precede outcome specification.
